careerProfile.title: Career Profile
careerProfile.subtitle: Quantitative educator transitioning applied mathematics expertise into analytics and AI product development

professionalBackground.title: Professional Background
paragraphs: Daniel Cohen is a Mathematics and Statistics professional with graduate credentials from Hunter College (M.S. Statistics), Baruch College (MBA Finance & Investment), and Queens College (B.A. Applied Mathematics & Economics). His career foundation is quantitative education — teaching mathematics and statistics at the high school and college levels and supporting students through academic tutoring centers.

That instructional background shaped a core strength: making complex quantitative concepts accessible without sacrificing rigor. Today that skill shows up in interactive dashboards, simulation labs, and case-study-driven portfolio storytelling built for recruiters and hiring managers.

analyticsAiTransition.title: Analytics & AI Transition
paragraphs: Over the past two years Daniel has built the Daniel AI Suite — seven deployed Streamlit applications plus SQL and Excel workbooks — as deliberate evidence of analytics and AI-assisted development capability.

The transition is not a pivot away from mathematics; it is an extension. Portfolio optimization, significance testing, Monte Carlo simulation, ML projections, and AI evaluation workflows all apply the same quantitative toolkit he has taught for years — now packaged as production decision-support products.

careerDirection.title: Career Direction
careerDirection.text: Daniel is targeting Data Analyst, AI Evaluator, Business Intelligence Analyst, Research Analyst, and Junior Data Scientist roles where quantitative reasoning, clear communication, and hands-on product development are valued. He brings a rare combination: credentialed statistics training, years of quantitative teaching, and a deployed portfolio that proves he can build — not just analyze.

aboutMe.headline: Mathematics & Statistics Professional · Data Analyst · Quantitative Educator
aboutMe.subheadline: AI-assisted application developer specializing in analytics, modeling, statistics, and decision-support systems
paragraphs: I am a Mathematics and Statistics professional who translates statistical theory, financial modeling, machine learning, and experimental design into interactive analytics products that help people make better decisions.

My work spans portfolio risk analysis, sports analytics, probability modeling, ML projections, data visualization, and AI-assisted software — with emphasis on experimentation, reproducibility, and business impact.
As a quantitative educator and builder, I design dashboards and simulations that make complex analysis accessible without sacrificing analytical rigor.

pillars.title: Analytics & Modeling
pillars.text: Hypothesis testing, regression, simulation, optimization, and scenario analysis across finance and sports domains.

pillars.title: Experimentation
pillars.text: Structured A/B-style evaluation, significance testing, and model validation workflows with interpretable outputs.

pillars.title: Decision Support
pillars.text: Interactive dashboards that connect data pipelines to actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.

pillars.title: AI-Assisted Development
pillars.text: Production Streamlit applications combining live APIs, cached analytics, and portfolio-grade UX polish.

featuredAnalytics.title: Machine Learning Projections

featuredAnalytics.title: Probability Modeling

featuredAnalytics.title: Scenario Simulations
featuredAnalytics.text: Monte Carlo paths, what-if sliders, and forward macro projections for risk planning.

featuredAnalytics.title: Data Visualization

summary.headline: Quantitative Analytics & AI Professional
paragraphs: I build analytics products that connect statistical reasoning, financial modeling, SQL and Excel reporting, and AI evaluation workflows into decision-ready applications.

My portfolio spans production Streamlit dashboards, SQL and Excel analytics workbooks, and a seven-app AI suite — demonstrating end-to-end ability from data modeling through recruiter-facing storytelling.
Target roles: Data Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, Research Analyst, Quantitative Analyst, Financial Analyst, AI Evaluator, and AI Trainer.

repos.name: Investment Portfolio Analyzer
repos.oneLiner: Live-market portfolio analytics with risk metrics, Monte Carlo simulation, and efficient frontier optimization.

detailSections.title: Portfolio Analytics
detailSections.body: Live holdings dashboard with allocation breakdown, growth curves, Sharpe/Sortino metrics, and benchmark comparison — designed for both beginner and advanced analyst workflows.

detailSections.title: Risk Analysis
detailSections.body: Correlation heatmaps, volatility ranking, risk contribution charts, and scenario stress views that translate portfolio theory into visual decision support.

detailSections.title: Allocation Modeling
detailSections.body: Preset strategy mixes, custom weight inputs, efficient frontier visualization, and optimizer outputs for max-Sharpe and min-volatility portfolios.

detailSections.title: Quantitative Decision Support
detailSections.body: Rule-based health scoring, Monte Carlo simulation, and macro-adjusted forward projections help users evaluate trade-offs without institutional terminals.

keyFeatures: Sharpe, Sortino, VaR, and drawdown analytics
detailHighlights: Portfolio analytics dashboard with allocation breakdown
Risk analysis: correlation heatmaps and contribution charts
Allocation modeling with preset strategies and custom weights

caseStudy.problem: Investors need accessible risk-adjusted analytics without institutional terminals or spreadsheet-only workflows.
caseStudy.data: Live equity/ETF prices via yfinance; user-defined holdings and benchmark returns.
caseStudy.analysis: Daily return series, extended risk metrics, benchmark comparison, and health diagnostics.
caseStudy.modeling: Monte Carlo simulation, mean-variance optimization, and efficient frontier construction.
caseStudy.insights: Rule-based portfolio insights flag concentration, volatility outliers, and diversification gaps.
caseStudy.results: Single Streamlit product delivering institutional-style analytics for educational and decision-support use.

repos.purpose: Streamlit dashboard for portfolio risk/return analysis with live Yahoo Finance data, Monte Carlo simulation, efficient frontier optimization, and rule-based insights.
repos.executiveSummary: Built a production Streamlit dashboard that pulls live market data and delivers institutional-grade portfolio analytics — risk metrics, Monte Carlo simulation, efficient frontier optimization, and rule-based insights.
repos.businessValue: Translates complex portfolio theory into an accessible tool for evaluating risk-adjusted returns, diversification, and allocation strategies.
repos.recruiterTakeaway: Can build end-to-end financial analytics products with real data, not just notebooks.
screenshotChecklist: investment-overview-demo.png — Overview with allocation, Sharpe, growth (P0, Demo Mode)
repos.readmeStatus: Strong — features, quick start, project structure, dev workflow. Missing screenshots and live demo URL.

Machine learning projections for star players

caseStudy.problem: Fantasy managers and analysts need rigorous statistical tools—not just leaderboards—to evaluate players and draft decisions.
caseStudy.data: Lahman historical database, FantasyPros market rankings, and derived projection features.
caseStudy.analysis: Trend slopes, R², volatility scoring, and significance tests on comparable season windows.
caseStudy.modeling: ML-style next-season projections with aging curves, regression-to-mean, and similar-player blending.
caseStudy.insights: Draft Fit Score combines market rank, scarcity, team needs, and ML projection signals.
caseStudy.results: Unified decision-support platform spanning exploration, testing, projection, and live draft simulation.

repos.recruiterTakeaway: Combines statistical rigor with product thinking — not just charts, but decision workflows.

repos.name: NBA Playoff Companion AI

detailSections.title: Matchup Analysis

detailSections.title: Scenario Simulation

keyFeatures: Home playoff command center with series probability
Live bracket sync with demo fallback
Legacy Tracker with player scoring ladders
Portfolio demo mode for Knicks Finals scenario
detailHighlights: Legacy scoring with historical comparison charts
Playoff forecasting and series probability modeling
Matchup analysis with rotation and intelligence panels

caseStudy.problem: Playoff fans need a single dashboard for bracket context, player impact, and forward-looking storylines—not scattered box scores.
caseStudy.data: NBA API playoff logs, curated bracket snapshots, and team/player profile metadata.
caseStudy.analysis: Round-by-round splits, emphasis cards, and matchup pulse summaries on the home dashboard.
caseStudy.modeling: Legacy score engine with slider-based what-if paths and franchise ladder comparisons.
caseStudy.insights: Series win probability pills and locked-vs-simulator legacy modes clarify actual vs projected impact.
caseStudy.results: Multi-page sports analytics product demonstrating live data resilience and fan-facing decision support.

caseStudy.problem: Musicians need structured practice tools that adapt to skill level, song structure, and harmonic context—not static PDF charts.
caseStudy.data: Curated song catalog, user chord charts, MIDI/MusicXML imports, and audio signal features.
caseStudy.analysis: Section-scoped practice recommendations, harmonic rhythm intelligence, and progression analysis.
caseStudy.modeling: Rule-based and AI-assisted backing generation; adaptive sheet difficulty scaling.
caseStudy.insights: Practice coach surfaces focus areas; backing studio matches groove, meter, and key to active song.
caseStudy.results: Nine-page creative analytics studio combining audio engineering, music theory, and AI-assisted UX.

repos.businessValue: Demonstrates rare ability to combine AI, audio engineering, and interactive product design in a cohesive creative tool.

screenshotChecklist: music-practice-demo.png — Practice studio full song card (P0)

sqlExcelWorkbooks.name: AI Evaluator & Analytics Combined Workbook
sqlExcelWorkbooks.executiveSummary: Combined AI evaluator practice workbook with 150-row evaluation dataset, prompt rating exercises, pairwise comparisons, 11 SQL analytics queries (intermediate to advanced), pandas equivalents, and a KPI dashboard.
sqlExcelWorkbooks.recruiterTakeaway: Practices the full AI evaluator workflow — rating, reasoning, and SQL-based performance analysis on the same dataset.

sqlExcelWorkbooks.name: Investment App Portfolio Analysis Workbook
sqlExcelWorkbooks.executiveSummary: Investment analyst workbook mirroring the Streamlit app workflow — allocations, Sharpe ratios, Monte Carlo scenarios (500 simulations), optimizer portfolios (251 candidates), 10 pivot exercises, 11 SQL queries, and dashboard storytelling tasks.
sqlExcelWorkbooks.recruiterTakeaway: Connects a live Python app to SQL and Excel analyst workflows — the same metrics, three different delivery formats.

sqlExcelWorkbooks.name: Quant Analytics Portfolio Workbook
sqlExcelWorkbooks.executiveSummary: Cross-domain quant workbook with 150 insurance claims, 60 baseball player records, statistical distribution reference, 10 SQL practice questions, 8 pivot exercises, and dashboard KPI ideas.
sqlExcelWorkbooks.recruiterTakeaway: Demonstrates quant thinking across insurance and sports — two domains, one analytical toolkit.

sqlExcelWorkbooks.name: Real World Data Analytics Portfolio Workbook
sqlExcelWorkbooks.executiveSummary: Real-world analytics workbook with 200-company credit risk dataset, 300 insurance claims with fraud flags, portfolio return time series, distribution practice, 10 SQL analytics tasks, and 6 portfolio project ideas.
sqlExcelWorkbooks.recruiterTakeaway: Works with realistic multi-domain datasets — credit, insurance, and market risk in one analytical portfolio.

sqlExcelWorkbooks.name: Actuarial Analytics Practice Workbook
sqlExcelWorkbooks.executiveSummary: Actuarial analytics practice workbook (PDF export available). Excel source file not present in portfolio folder — PDF serves as reference documentation.
